title: Chemical equilibria
summary: 9701 AS equilibrium — dynamic equilibrium, Kc, and Le Chatelier's principle with PhET acid-base equilibrium visualisation.
learningObjectives: Explain dynamic equilibrium in closed systems.
Write Kc expressions and calculate equilibrium constants.
Apply Le Chatelier's principle to predict equilibrium shifts.
Distinguish Kc from reaction quotient Q.

simpleExplanation.title: Balance at the molecular level
simpleExplanation.summary: At equilibrium, forward and reverse rates are equal and concentrations stay constant — but reactions never stop. Changing conditions shifts the position to partially oppose the change.
steps: Reversible reactions: ⇌ symbol; both directions occur.
Dynamic equilibrium: closed system, constant temperature, rates equal.
Le Chatelier: increase [product] → shift left; increase temperature favours endothermic direction.
Kc = products/reactants (each to power of coefficient); only T changes Kc.

sections.content: **Equilibrium** appears on every 9701 AS paper — Kc calculations, **Le Chatelier** predictions, and **industrial conditions** (Haber, Contact process) are exam staples.
For aA + bB ⇌ cC + dD: Kc = [C]^c[D]^d / [A]^a[B]^b | Omit solids and pure liquids

sections.question: N₂O₄ ⇌ 2NO₂. At equilibrium [N₂O₄] = 0.40 mol dm⁻³, [NO₂] = 0.80 mol dm⁻³. Find Kc.

sections.content: When predicting shifts, state **which side is favoured** and **why** (Le Chatelier). For Kp questions, use partial pressures and the same expression form as Kc.
